title: Anthony O'Daly
content:
Since your limbs were laid out
            The stars do not shine,
        The fish leap not out
            In the waves.
        On our meadows the dew
            Does not fall in the morn,
        For O'Daly is dead:
            Not a flower can be born,
        Not a word can be said,
            Not a tree have a leaf;
        Anthony, after you
            There is nothing to do,
        There is nothing but grief.
meaning:
James Stephens's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Anthony O'Daly"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
